items.rationale: His central thesis (and book): NFTs give people ownership/identity, bridge physical-digital, and let brands build community; he sees them becoming everyday brand infrastructure, not just art.
items.age_note: Stated since 2023-12 and continuously restated through 2025-03; current and reinforced, not stale.

citations.quote: NFTs are poised to become ubiquitous brand assets in 2024
three steps of the NFT staircase -- ownership, utility, and identity -- within fewer than 60 seconds of opening the pack

items.rationale: He views IP as a huge, market-failure-ridden market and sees an on-chain programmable IP layer (esp. Story Protocol) as the most exciting infrastructure innovation he's seen in 15 years, especially given generative-AI remixing.

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol
I'm extremely excited see what having a programmable onchain IP layer can unlock

items.rationale: He believes user ownership of data plus cross-platform wallet portability can disrupt monolithic web platforms, intensify competition, and shift network effects to assets/protocols rather than walled gardens.
items.age_note: Core stance from 2024-01 restated through 2025-01; persistent and current.

citations.quote: Web3 has the potential to disrupt the monolithic power of today's top web platforms. It's not inevitable, but it's definitely real.
blockchains... give people ownership and portability of their digital assets has the potential to significantly increase web platform competition

items.rationale: Cheap, high-throughput L2 transactions make large-scale, low-friction onchain collectibles and consumer experiences finally viable — a precondition he flagged years earlier now realized.

citations.quote: once transaction fees got small enough, blockchains would enable all manner of low-cost digital collecting experiences

items.rationale: His 2025 a16z 'Big Idea' is an emerging renaissance of information aggregation; he repeatedly engages prediction markets and strategic-voting/pivotality as serious, valuable mechanisms.
items.age_note: Most recent (Dec 2024) framing; freshest signal in this area.

citations.quote: Mine for this year is about the emerging renaissance of information aggregation
Had a great time talking prediction markets and beyond with... @ATabarrok and @smc90!

items.rationale: He pushes a rigorous framework for token classification and dynamic monetary policy, treating tokenomics as evolving market design rather than fixed-at-launch parameters.
items.age_note: Token-definition framework is his most recent crypto output (Mar 2025); current.

citations.quote: not all tokens are created equal... a framework for how a token's economic and network foundations shape its value and legal treatment
With token design, monetary policy evolving over time should be the default.

items.rationale: He wants competition in social media and sees decentralized, protocol-based social (e.g., Farcaster) plus onchain identity verification against AI fakes as the path.

citations.quote: The need for competition in the social media market is a big part of why I'm excited about decentralized social media protocols

items.rationale: Long-standing fascination (from work with Gary Becker) with sharing research returns to patients; sees blockchain solving infrastructure/access barriers in health-data marketplaces.

citations.quote: Many ideas for these marketplaces have been explored, but they've faced fundamental infrastructure and access challenges -- which @Amino_Chain is using blockchain... to hopefully solve

items.rationale: He repeatedly argues that vague enforcement-driven rules harm innovation; favors clear, principles-based regulation (FIT21) so builders aren't left guessing what's a security.

citations.quote: maybe we should end regulation-by-enforcement
FIT21 is a huge step forward for blockchain and crypto technology

items.rationale: His research argues platforms are structurally ill-positioned to solve misinformation; well-meaning moderation can backfire when users distrust the platform — they should target harm-enabling content instead.

citations.quote: it's not one that platforms aren't well-positioned to solve... attempts to moderate misinformation can backfire

items.rationale: He warns that copying the Bitcoin precedent of fixing token supply/emissions up front is usually wrong because money demand and ecosystem dynamics change over time.

citations.quote: Owing in part to the Bitcoin precedent, many projects try to fix their entire tokenomic design... But that's rarely the right path.

items.rationale: He concedes much of current web3 is unsustainable or outright fraudulent — like the early Internet — and stresses the need for frameworks supporting high-quality actors.
items.age_note: From Jan 2024, reaffirmed Aug 2024; persists as a measured caveat alongside his bullishness.

citations.quote: many of the business models people have been experimenting with are unsustainable at best, and outright fraudulent at worst

items.rationale: He flags rising account compromise and AI-generated fakes as a first-order risk, arguing for self-custodied, interoperable onchain identity assets you alone control.

citations.quote: we're seeing increasingly many issues with account compromise in both infrastructure providers and application platforms like social media

items.rationale: He is dismissive of memecoins as low-substance, viral-by-nature instruments — explicitly likening them to chain emails rather than value-creating assets.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.

items.rationale: He warns these are categorically different from genuine network tokens and conflating them misleads on value and legal treatment.

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens are *not* network tokens. Here's a framework for understanding the difference

risk_regime.rationale: As an a16z crypto researcher and active builder/collector, he is structurally bullish on web3/NFTs/tokens/IP infrastructure as genuinely disruptive — while explicitly hedging that the upside is 'real' but 'not inevitable' and that today's market is immature. Net: long-horizon risk-on with eyes open, not speculative euphoria.

notes: Kominers' public corpus is dominated by personal/NFT-collecting and Covid-policy content; market views were extracted from his a16z/professional statements. He rarely endorses specific tokens (hence empty tokens_excited) — his enthusiasm attaches to categories, infrastructure (esp. Story Protocol), and mechanisms, consistent with his market-design lens. His excitement is remarkably stable and continuously restated 2023-12 through 2025-03, so most entries are current/grounded rather than persisted-but-stale. Concerns are framed as design/regulatory caveats within an overall constructive posture rather than bearishness. Pervasive non-market themes (Covid mitigation, math/puzzles, recreational NFT collecting) were excluded as not constituting market views.
